title: Java HTTP/3 Client
description: Send a request over HTTP/3 with the Java 26 HttpClient.
learningGoals: Opt in to HTTP/3 by setting HttpClient.Version.HTTP_3 (JEP 517, final in Java 26).
Send a request and read back which protocol version the server actually used.

completionChecks.prompt: Which HTTP protocol version does the client builder request?
completionChecks.explanation: The builder sets version(HttpClient.Version.HTTP_3), asking the client to use HTTP/3 when available.

completionChecks.prompt: What does response.version() print after the request completes?
choices: Always HTTP_3, even if the server negotiated a fallback
The protocol version actually used for the response
completionChecks.explanation: The request asks for HTTP/3, but response.version() reports the negotiated protocol that the server and client actually used.

completionChecks.prompt: Which statement separates this Java 26 HTTP/3 lesson from preview APIs?
choices: HTTP/3 support is requested through HttpClient.Version.HTTP_3 and is final in Java 26
It requires --enable-preview because HttpClient is preview-only
It replaces URI with URL in all requests
It can only send POST requests
completionChecks.explanation: HTTP/3 support is a final Java 26 HttpClient feature. Preview flags are not the mechanism; the code opts in through the client version.
